answer: I am drawn to Discord because of its commitment to fostering community and connection through innovative technology. answer: I learned about this job opportunity at Discord through a professional network where industry colleagues frequently share openings. Additionally, I follow Discord's updates and initiatives, which keeps me informed about roles that align with my skills and interests in product management and community engagement.

answer: As the first Business Operations & Strategy hire, you're looking for a generalist who can own pricing, strategic planning, and financial modeling while standing up new functions from scratch — which is exactly the work I've been doing. At M Science I led a cross-functional overhaul of our pricing and packaging model and stood up a pricing desk that drove a ~35% ARR increase in its first quarter, then built the financial and data infrastructure to make it repeatable. I'm drawn to Hang because it lets me do that end-to-end, working directly with the CEO on a consumer-brand platform I'd genuinely enjoy helping build.

question: Why are you interested in joining Tailscale? Thoughtful responses are encouraged.*
answer:
What draws me to Tailscale is the shape of the BizOps & Strategy mandate: being the embedded operator for the cross-functional problems that don't have a natural owner, and owning them from diagnosis through a durable, handed-off solution. That is exactly how I have worked. At M Science I led a cross-functional overhaul of our packaging and pricing model — aligning data science, sales, and research, standing up a pricing desk, and driving a ~35% ARR increase the quarter after launch — then built the infrastructure to make it stick: a Salesforce master-data system that unified our product metadata and automated entitlements, serving as the semantic layer behind our analytics and customer portal. I like building those structures early so they can be handed off as a company scales.

The specific problems in this role map directly to my experience — pricing and packaging strategy, maturing GTM systems alongside RevOps, reducing rework in business systems, and doing my own data analysis in SQL and Python rather than waiting on someone else. I am also genuinely excited by a PLG, developer-facing company: earning adoption bottom-up rewards exactly the kind of quantitative, first-principles operator I try to be. The written, async culture and high tolerance for ambiguity suit how I work best — I do my sharpest thinking when I get to define the problem, not just answer a scoped one. Tailscale's next chapter, from international/APAC expansion to cross-company AI adoption, is full of the ambiguous, high-leverage problems I want to spend my time on.

question: We're always curious - have you used Tailscale before?*
answer: I haven’t used it, but I’m excited to learn more!

answer: Closest to product operations: I own the unit-economics model for M Science's KPI API product line, weighing operational and tooling spend against ARR, and I partner with sales and marketing leadership on go-to-market spend decisions. I have not formally owned a headcount or marketing OpEx budget line in a dedicated FP&A capacity.

question: What’s one useful thing you built / improved with AI recently? Feel free to be brief. *
answer: I built an automated data-QA layer for our KPI API product using an OCR/LLM pipeline plus ML anomaly detection, which cut the product's annual data error rate from over 2% to near zero.
